subject: Math

game_theme.title: Pattern Harbor
game_theme.mission_intro: Use multiplicative comparison and patterns to help the Skill World crew solve a real Grade 4 math challenge.

lesson.story_mission: Join the Pattern Harbor crew and solve problems about understand multiplicative comparison, factors, multiples, prime and composite numbers, and number or shape patterns.
lesson.mini_lesson: In this lesson, you will understand multiplicative comparison, factors, multiples, prime and composite numbers, and number or shape patterns. Use models to keep the math organized and explain each answer.
lesson.objective: Understand multiplicative comparison, factors, multiples, prime and composite numbers, and number or shape patterns.
lesson.concept_explanation: Grade 4 mathematicians use visual models and precise vocabulary to understand multiplicative comparison, factors, multiples, prime and composite numbers, and number or shape patterns.
lesson.common_misconception: A common mistake is using the right numbers but the wrong relationship, place value, or rule.
lesson.real_world_connection: These skills help with planning, checking totals, comparing quantities, and solving multi-step real-world problems.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read carefully, model the relationship, calculate with place value, and check for reasonableness.

audio.text: In this lesson, we practice Multiplicative Comparison and Patterns. Use the visuals, listen to the page narration, and explain why your answer works.
audio.label: Listen to Lesson

worked_examples.prompt: 6 is how many times as many as 3?
hints: Underline the math action in the question.
Use the visual model to organize place value and relationships.
Check that your answer matches the question and is reasonable.

feedback.correct: Yes! Your reasoning matches the model.
feedback.incorrect: Try again by using the model and checking each place.

additive_vs_multiplicative_confusion.description: Watch for additive vs multiplicative confusion.

story.text: Welcome to Multiplicative Comparison and Patterns. This mission connects real math to a clear visual model before you solve on your own. Press Start Mission when you are ready to read the problem, notice what the model is showing, and explain your thinking.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This page teaches the main ideas for Multiplicative Comparison and Patterns. Read the objective, vocabulary, misconception warning, and real-world connection so you know what to watch for. Press Next when you can name the strategy you will use.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Watch this example as a tutor would solve it. Step one is to read the question and identify the operation or pattern. Step two is to use the visual model to organize the numbers. Step three is to compute carefully and check that the final answer is reasonable. Press Next when you can explain why the answer is correct.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This guided demo gives you support before independent work. Read each part of the model, connect it to the lesson vocabulary, and say what each number means. Press Next when the reasoning feels clear.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Now try a practice question with support. Tap Read Question first, choose your answer or type it carefully, and use the hint if your first idea does not fit the model. Press Next after your answer is complete.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This challenge asks you to use the skill with less help. Tap Read Question, decide which strategy fits, and check your answer against the visual model. Press Next when you can justify your response.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This checkpoint shows what you can do independently. Tap Read Question, answer carefully, and use the model to verify your reasoning before you move on. Press Next after your best work.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Great work completing Multiplicative Comparison and Patterns. This badge celebrates careful math reasoning, clear models, and answers that match the question. Press Save Growth Data to add this skill to your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: Your profile summarizes this mission and points you to more practice. Choose Continue to Skill Practice for focused levels and a Mixed level, or go back to the hub when you are ready.
profile.label: Read This Page
